name: TypeBox
what_it_does: TypeScript runtime type library that builds standard JSON Schema definitions with TypeScript types inferred simultaneously. A single TypeBox schema gives you both the TypeScript type and JSON Schema for validation. Uses AJV for JSON Schema validation (fastest JS validator). Used extensively with Fastify (official Fastify integration) for request/response validation with zero overhead on the server. Bridge between TypeScript's compile-time types and JSON Schema's runtime validation.
use_cases: Define API schemas once with TypeBox and get TypeScript types + JSON Schema validation simultaneously
Integrate with Fastify's schema validation system using TypeBox for type-safe, high-performance route validation
Generate JSON Schema definitions from TypeScript-style definitions for OpenAPI documentation and contract-first APIs
Replace Zod in performance-sensitive applications — TypeBox+AJV validation is significantly faster than Zod's runtime checks
Build type-safe serialization/deserialization where the same schema validates incoming requests and types outgoing responses
not_for: Teams prioritizing DX over performance — Zod's API is more ergonomic and has better TypeScript inference for complex schemas
Client-side form validation — Zod integrates better with react-hook-form and Yup is more form-focused
JSON Schema-unaware environments — TypeBox's JSON Schema output requires AJV or another JSON Schema validator
best_when: You need maximum validation performance (Fastify APIs, high-throughput servers) and want TypeScript types derived from JSON Schema definitions.
avoid_when: You prefer Zod's ergonomic DX — TypeBox has higher performance but Zod has better TypeScript inference for complex schema transformations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AJV validation errors include JSON pointer paths to failing properties. TypeBox schema compilation errors are compile-time TypeScript errors. Error format follows JSON Schema specification.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Schema compilation and validation are deterministic — same schema + same data = same validation result.
known_agent_gotchas: TypeBox schemas are JSON Schema — complex TypeScript patterns (conditional types, mapped types) don't translate; use TypeBox's built-in schema combinators
The Encode/Decode feature (TypeBox value transformations) is separate from basic validation — don't confuse validation with transformation
AJV must be added as a separate dependency — TypeBox generates JSON Schema but doesn't bundle AJV for validation
TypeBox 0.32+ has significant API changes from 0.28 — breaking changes in schema construction APIs require migration
T.Object strict mode (additionalProperties: false) doesn't apply by default — must explicitly set to prevent extra properties from passing validation
Generic schemas require TypeBox's generic schema utilities — TypeScript generics don't automatically translate to reusable TypeBox generics

security_score_components.security_notes: Schema validation library — no network calls. AJV validation prevents injection via strict schema enforcement. MIT licensed.
